current_question: ANSWERED: 5 structural failures + transition likely first-order. DQCP ruled out (~95%).
questions_answered: Define both phases precisely (iter 1-2: fracton = type-I topological order; condensate = [U(1)]^3 Higgs)
Condensate phase characterized (iter 2: D_{r,i}, graviton Goldstone, linearized GR)
Analogy dictionary (iter 3: 4/12 no match, 3/12 strained — analogy forced)
Fracton phase: type-I topological order, GSD~exp(L), Wilson surfaces (iter 3)
Transition order: CONTESTED — 50% first-order (CW+HLM), 30% continuous (Lifshitz z=2), 15% crossover (iter 4)
VERDICT: DQCP RULED OUT at 95% — structural failures sufficient regardless of transition order
open_questions: RESOLVED: transition order genuinely uncertain but moot for DQCP verdict
OPEN: is z=2 at criticality? (needs lattice simulation)
OPEN: rank-2 CW calculation appears novel/unpublished
OPEN: effective charge of dipole Higgs (F-S applicability)
evidence_for: Two genuinely incompatible phases exist (topological vs symmetry-broken)
Fractionalized excitations (fractons) exist in the theory
Condensate phase precisely characterized with explicit order parameter D_{r,i} = v
h_{ij} = A_{ij} - d_(i theta_j) has exact linearized diffeo invariance
Mean-field: no cubic invariants, second-order at tree level
Lifshitz z=2 defense: d_eff=5 > d_c=4 → CW corrections irrelevant (genuine argument)
Fradkin-Shenker does NOT apply to rank-2 (dipole can't screen fracton charges)
evidence_against: FATAL: 5/6 structural DQCP criteria fail (topological order, deconfinement, emergent gauge, Berry phase, dimensionality)
STRONG: CW mechanism with N_gauge~5-7 massive modes favors first-order
STRONG: HLM mechanism 1.3-1.5x stronger than standard SC
STRONG: All known z=1 gauge-Higgs systems in 3+1D are first-order
MODERATE: Crystal melting duality predicts first-order (but z=2 may change this)
STRONG: Analogy dictionary 4/12 no match, 3/12 strained
MODERATE: At strong coupling, instantons can override Lifshitz protection
learnings: Standard DQCP requires confinement on BOTH sides — hardest criterion
3+1D fundamentally different from 2+1D — Polyakov doesn't apply
Dipole field D_{r,i} carries charge 1 under U(1)_i (proven by lattice calc)
S-wave is the ONLY partial wave consistent with isotropic GR
CW: N_gauge~5-7 massive modes, 2x stronger than scalar QED
LIFSHITZ DEFENSE: z=2 → d_eff=5 > d_c=4 → CW irrelevant in RG sense
Lifshitz self-consistent: above d_c, z is not renormalized
Both g^4~1/xi^2 and lambda~1/xi decay, but g^4 faster → CW barrier parametrically smaller
F-S does NOT apply to rank-2: dipole can't screen fracton charges (charge>1 analog)
Subsystem symmetries obstruct F-S analytic continuation between phases
FDCG itself is UNAFFECTED by DQCP ruling — condensate phase description holds
key_calculations: Pretko action: S = int [1/2g_e^-2(d_0 A_ij)^2 - 1/2g_m^-2(d_i A_jk - d_j A_ik)^2]
Gauss: d_id_j E^ij = rho → dipole conservation
Wilson surface: W(Sigma) = exp(i int_Sigma A_ij dS^ij)
Metric: h_ij = A_ij - d_(i theta_j) → linearized diffeo
GL potential: V = r|D|^2 + u(|D|^2)^2 + v Sum|D_i|^4 — NO cubics
Lifshitz: z=2 → d_eff=5, g is RG-irrelevant → CW suppressed
